dimensions.evidence: Requeue guidance: 'do NOT create a second copy under internal/engine/' — diff creates internal/engine/language_tiers.go anyway; KnownLanguages in detect.go not generic.go; only 9/22 language IDs in the CI guard slice, missing 13 languages from the plan's explicit list.
Files created in wrong package (engine/ vs extending language_tiers/); detect.go modified instead of generic.go as plan specified.
KnownLanguages() returns a hardcoded 9-ID slice rather than iterating discovery.extToLanguage; new extensions added to extToLanguage won't fail CI, defeating the plan's core enforcement goal.

Creates a competing tier implementation at engine/ when language_tiers/ already exists; CI guard is structurally weakened and won't catch new extensions; no immediate crash risk but architectural debt is material.

failureReasons: Requeue guidance explicitly forbids creating files under internal/engine/ — the work should EXTEND the existing internal/language_tiers/ package. Diff creates new internal/engine/language_tiers.go and _test.go in direct violation.
KnownLanguages() placed in detect.go instead of the specified generic.go, and returns only 9 hardcoded IDs instead of iterating extToLanguage or covering all 22 registered languages.

CI guard test iterates a hardcoded 9-language slice rather than the actual discovery extToLanguage map, meaning new extensions added to extToLanguage will NOT cause a test failure — the enforcement is structurally gamed.
Registry map contains 22 entries but KnownLanguages() exposes only 9, creating inconsistency between what's registered and what's tested.
